methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $274M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.5B.

data.narrative:
Sift is a Series B-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Karthik Gollapudi and Austin Spiegel, currently valued at $274M post-money on $65M total raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers at same or adjacent stage (including Aalyria at $1.3B, Varda Space Industries at $1.5B, and Impulse Space at $1.8B) and 5 public Space & Aerospace benchmarks (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-Progression anchored to the $274M current valuation applies a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implying $521M. Peer-Median across the 8-company comparable set yields a median valuation of $1.2B, with a range spanning $400M to $2.5B. Public comp anchoring cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue figures. These two methods yield a triangulated range: low of $443M, median of $873M, and high of $1.4B.

The peer-median method suggests materially higher valuations than stage-progression, reflecting the strength of recent funding rounds in the sector. Key uncertainties include Sift's specific product maturity relative to Series C peers and revenue trajectory relative to the public comps' 59.8x median P/S multiple. The $873M median represents a reasonable anchor pending additional performance data.
